—“The Lord is my shepherd.” In these words, a believer is taught to express satisfaction in the care of the great Pastor of the universe, the Redeemer and Preserver of men. With joy he reflects that he has a shepherd, and that shepherd is Jehovah. A flock of sheep, gentle and harmless, feeding in green pastures, under the care of a skillful, watchful, and tender shepherd, forms an image of believers brought back to the Shepherd of their souls. The greatest abundance means nothing to a wicked man, who enjoys only what pleases his senses; but to a godly man, who by faith tastes the goodness of God in all his enjoyments, even if he has little of the world, it is a green pasture. The Lord gives peace and contentment of mind, whatever one’s circumstances are. If we are blessed with the green pastures of the ordinances, let us not think it enough to simply pass through them, but let us abide in them. The consolations of the Holy Spirit are the still waters by which the saints are led; the streams that flow from the Fountain of living waters. Those only are led by the still waters of comfort who walk in the paths of righteousness. The way of duty is the truly pleasant way. Righteous work brings peace. In these paths we cannot walk unless God leads us into them and guides us on in them. Discontent and distrust come from unbelief; an unsteady walk is the result: let us then simply trust our Shepherd’s care, and listen to his voice. The valley of the shadow of death may describe the most severe and terrible affliction, or dark event that the psalmist ever experienced. Between those of the flock on earth and those who have gone to heaven, death lies like a dark valley that must be passed in going from one to the other. But even in this there are words which lessen the fear. It is but the shadow of death: the shadow of a serpent will not sting, nor the shadow of a sword kill. It is a valley, deep indeed, and dark, and muddy; but valleys are often fruitful, and so is death itself fruitful of comforts to God’s people. It is a walk through it: they will not be lost in this valley, but get safely to the mountain on the other side. Death is a king of terrors, but not to the sheep of Christ. When they come to die, God will rebuke the enemy; he will guide them with his rod, and sustain them with his staff. There is enough in the gospel to comfort believers when dying, and underneath them are everlasting arms. The Lord’s people feast at his table, upon the provisions of his love. Satan and wicked men are not able to destroy their comforts while they are anointed with the Holy Spirit, and drink of the cup of salvation which is always full. Past experience teaches believers to trust that the goodness and mercy of God will follow them all the days of their lives, and it is their desire and determination to seek their happiness in the service of God here, and they hope to enjoy his love forever in heaven. While here, the Lord can make any situation pleasant by the anointing of his Spirit and the joys of his salvation. But those who want to be satisfied with the blessings of his house must stay close to its duties.
